tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: DCIS Endocrine Risk Reduction: Tamoxifen
tier_1_regimens.notes: Tamoxifen for 5 years in ER-positive DCIS treated with BCS ± RT reduces risk of ipsilateral and contralateral breast cancer recurrence. NSABP B-24 showed 3.4% absolute reduction in ipsilateral recurrence and 3.2% reduction in contralateral breast cancers with tamoxifen vs placebo at median follow-up of 13.6 years (HR 0.30 for ipsilateral, HR 0.68 for contralateral). [23]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: DCIS Endocrine Risk Reduction: Low-Dose Tamoxifen
tier_1_regimens.notes: Low-dose tamoxifen (5 mg/day for 3 years) is an option if standard 20 mg dose is not tolerated. TAM-01 trial showed recurrence rate of 5.7% with tamoxifen 5 mg daily vs 11.9% with placebo (HR 0.48; 95% CI, 0.25–0.89). [61] Low-dose tamoxifen (10 mg every other day) is noted since 5-mg dose is not available in the US.

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: DCIS Endocrine Risk Reduction: Aromatase Inhibitor
tier_1_regimens.guideline_category: Preferred (alternative to tamoxifen in postmenopausal patients)
tier_1_regimens.notes: Anastrozole demonstrated non-inferiority to tamoxifen for prevention in IBIS-II DCIS trial. NSABP B-35 showed anastrozole reduced breast cancer-free interval events vs tamoxifen (HR 0.73; 95% CI, 0.56–0.96; P = .0234) in postmenopausal patients with HR-positive DCIS treated with lumpectomy and radiation. Some advantage for AI in patients <60 years or with thromboembolism concerns. [62,63]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: DCIS: Whole Breast RT After BCS
tier_1_regimens.notes: WBRT following BCS reduces IBTR rates for all DCIS by 50%–70%. Meta-analysis of 4 trials confirmed IBTR reduction with HR 0.49 (95% CI 0.41–0.58, P < .00001). Approx half recurrences are invasive, half DCIS. Risk of local relapse increases with larger DCIS, palpable mass, grade III, margins ≤2 mm, ER-negative tumors, and age <50. Omission of RT considered only if all RTOG 9804 criteria met: screen-detected, grade 1–2, size ≤2.5 cm, margins ≥3 mm. [22-27]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Chemotherapy: Dose-Dense AC Followed by Paclitaxel Every 2 Weeks

drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 14 days for 4 cycles, followed by Paclitaxel 175 mg/m² IV Day 1 cycled every 14 days for 4 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: Dose-dense AC followed by paclitaxel every 2 weeks. Cycles are with myeloid growth factor support. Shows survival benefit when compared with AC followed by every-3-week paclitaxel. A 26% reduction in hazard of recurrence (P = .01) and 31% reduction in hazard of death (P = .013) for dose-dense regimens. [1,384]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Chemotherapy: Dose-Dense AC Followed by Weekly Paclitaxel

drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 14 days for 4 cycles, followed by Paclitaxel 80 mg/m² IV weekly for 12 weeks

tier_1_regimens.notes: Weekly paclitaxel was superior to every-3-week paclitaxel in DFS (HR, 1.27; 95% CI, 1.03–1.57; P = .006) and OS (HR, 1.32; 95% CI, 1.02–1.72; P = .01). Cycles with myeloid growth factor support. [1,385]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Chemotherapy: TC (Docetaxel/Cyclophosphamide)

drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 4–6 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: At median follow-up of 7 years, overall DFS (81% vs 75%; HR, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.56–0.98; P = .033) and OS (87% vs 82%; HR, 0.69; 95% CI, 0.50–0.97; P = .032) were significantly improved with TC compared with AC. Non-anthracycline regimen preferred for patients where anthracyclines are contraindicated. [2,386]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Olaparib for gBRCA1/2 PV
tier_1_regimens.notes: 4-year iDFS was 82.7% with olaparib vs 75.4% with placebo (95% CI 3.0%–11.5%) and 4-year distant DFS was 86.5% vs 79.1% (95% CI 3.6%–11.3%). OS: 4-year OS 89.8% vs 86.4% (95% CI −0.1%–6.8%). Adjuvant olaparib can be given concurrently with endocrine therapy. [7,373]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Pembrolizumab for High-Risk TNBC (Post-Preoperative Therapy)

biomarkers.requirement: Not required for adjuvant if given in preoperative setting

tier_1_regimens.notes: For high-risk patients (ypT1–4,N0 or ypN≥1) with pembrolizumab given in preoperative setting. 5-year EFS: 81.3% with chemo+pembrolizumab vs 72.3% with placebo (HR 0.63; 95% CI 0.49-0.81). Among those with pCR who received adjuvant pembrolizumab, 5-year EFS was 92.2% vs 88.2% with chemo alone. [273,274]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Capecitabine for Residual Disease After Preoperative Chemotherapy
tier_1_regimens.site: Breast, invasive, HR-/HER2- (TNBC) with residual disease

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily on Days 1–14, cycled every 21 days for 6–8 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: CREATE-X evaluated adjuvant capecitabine in patients with residual invasive disease after standard preoperative chemotherapy. Improved DFS (HR 0.70, 95% CI 0.53-0.92, P = .01) and OS (HR for death 0.59, 95% CI 0.39-0.90, P = .01). OS was higher in TNBC (HR for death 0.52). [6,215]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Capecitabine Maintenance Therapy for TNBC

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days for 1 year

tier_1_regimens.notes: Maintenance low-dose capecitabine improved 5-year DFS in TNBC: 85.8% vs 75.8% (HR 0.60; 95% CI 0.38-0.92; P = .02). 5-year OS 85.5% vs 81.3% (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.47-1.19; P = .22). [398]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant TCHP (Docetaxel/Carboplatin + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab)

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 6 cycles

drugs.name: Pertuzumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 6 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: TCHP is a preferred regimen for HER2-positive disease. High pCR rates with pertuzumab-containing regimens (57%–66% in TRYPHAENA). After surgery, complete up to 1 year of HER2-targeted therapy. [28,270,403]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant AC Followed by TCH (Docetaxel/Carboplatin + Trastuzumab)

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.dose: 4 mg/kg loading then 2 mg/kg weekly (or 6 mg/kg every 21 days)
drugs.schedule: Cycled every 21 days for 6 cycles, followed by trastuzumab to complete 1 year

tier_1_regimens.notes: BCIRG 006 showed AC-TH and TCH were both superior to AC-T without trastuzumab. TCH had significantly lower cardiac toxicity (9.4% vs 18.6% LVEF decline, P < .0001; CHF 0.4% vs 2%, P < .001). TCH preferred for patients with cardiac risk factors. [27,403]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Paclitaxel + Trastuzumab (TCH-free)

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.schedule: To complete 1 year of treatment (or 6 mg/kg every 21 days as alternative)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Preferred for low-risk T1,N0,M0 HER2-positive disease, particularly those not eligible for other standard adjuvant regimens due to comorbidities. 3-year DFS was 98.7% (95% CI 97.6–99.8). 10-year invasive DFS 97.0% (95% CI 95.2-98.7%), 10-year breast cancer-specific survival 98.8%, 10-year OS 94.3%. Heart failure rate 0.5%. [26,404,405]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab + Chemotherapy (APHINITY)

drugs.name: Pertuzumab
drugs.schedule: With chemotherapy then maintenance with trastuzumab to complete 1 year

tier_1_regimens.notes: Updated results with median follow-up of 11.3 years confirmed benefit of adding pertuzumab to trastuzumab plus chemotherapy in node-positive disease: 8-year invasive DFS 86% vs 81% (HR 0.72; 95% CI 0.60-0.87). No benefit seen in node-negative subgroup. [408,409]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for Residual Disease (HER2-Positive)
tier_1_regimens.setting: Adjuvant after preoperative therapy with residual disease

drugs.name: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 14 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: For those with high risk of recurrence: defined as inoperable cancer (cT4, N0–3, M0 or cT1–3, N2–3, M0) at presentation or operable cancer (cT1–3, N0–1, M0) with axillary node–positive disease (ypN1–3) following preoperative therapy. Associated with ILD/pneumonitis risk; regular monitoring recommended. [30]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Ado-Trastuzumab Emtansine (T-DM1) for Residual Disease (HER2-Positive)
tier_1_regimens.setting: Adjuvant after preoperative therapy with residual disease

drugs.name: Ado-trastuzumab emtansine
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 14 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: KATHERINE trial: 3-year invasive DFS 88.3% with T-DM1 vs 77.0% with trastuzumab (HR 0.50, 95% CI 0.39-0.64, P < .001). If T-DM1 discontinued for toxicity, complete up to 1 year of HER2-directed therapy with trastuzumab ± pertuzumab. [410]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Abemaciclib + Endocrine Therapy

drugs.name: Abemaciclib
drugs.schedule: Twice daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days for 2 years

tier_1_regimens.notes: 2 years of adjuvant abemaciclib combined with endocrine therapy. For high-risk breast cancer defined as: ≥4 positive lymph nodes confirmed preoperatively and/or at surgery, or 1–3 positive lymph nodes with either grade 3 disease or tumor size ≥5 cm. Reduced absolute risk of recurrence at 4 years by 6.4% (HR 0.664, 95% CI 0.578-0.762, P < .0001). VTE risk should be considered when combining with tamoxifen. [369]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Ribociclib + Endocrine Therapy

drugs.name: Ribociclib
drugs.schedule: Once daily on Days 1–21, cycled every 28 days for 3 years

tier_1_regimens.notes: For patients with any lymph node involvement (excluding microscopic nodal involvement), or if no nodal involvement, either tumor size >5 cm, or tumor size 2–5 cm either grade 2 (and high genomic risk or Ki-67 ≥20%) or grade 3. Invasive DFS: 3.3% improvement (HR 0.75, 95% CI 0.62-0.91, P = .003). [4,372]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Systemic Therapy First-Line for HR+/HER2- Metastatic: AI + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Ribociclib)
tier_1_regimens.notes: MONALEESA-2: PFS 25.3 vs 16.0 months (HR 0.56; 95% CI 0.45–0.70). OS: 63.9 vs 51.4 months (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.63–0.93). MONALEESA-7 (pre/peri-menopausal): PFS 23.8 vs 13.0 months. OS: 58.7 vs 48.0 months (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.61–0.96). QTcF monitoring required. [499,501,502,503]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Systemic Therapy First-Line for HR+/HER2- Metastatic: AI + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Abemaciclib)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: median not reached vs 14.7 months (HR 0.54; 95% CI 0.41–0.72). ORR 59% vs 44%. Final OS analysis: median 66.8 vs 53.7 months (HR 0.80; 95% CI 0.64–1.02), no statistical significance. ILD/pneumonitis reported in 5.2%. [504,505]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Systemic Therapy First-Line for HR+/HER2- Metastatic: AI + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Palbociclib)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS 24.8 vs 14.5 months (HR 0.58; 95% CI 0.46–0.72). Final OS (90.1-month median follow-up) showed no statistically significant difference: 53.9 vs 51.2 months (HR 0.96; 95% CI 0.78–1.2). Neutropenia 66.5%. [506,507]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant/Inavolisib/Palbociclib for PIK3CA-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (progression on adjuvant ET or relapse within 12 months)

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 15.0 vs 7.3 months (HR 0.43; 95% CI 0.32–0.59). Median OS: 34 vs 27 months (HR 0.67; 95% CI 0.48–0.94). Higher rates of neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, hyperglycemia, and stomatitis with inavolisib. [510,511]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Ribociclib) for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second-line or after progression on adjuvant ET

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 21 vs 13 months (HR 0.59; 95% CI 0.48–0.73). At 42 months: estimated OS 57.8% (95% CI 52.0–63.2) vs 45.9% (95% CI 36.9–54.5). OS benefit demonstrated. [512,513]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + CDK4/6 Inhibitor (Abemaciclib) for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second-line or after progression on ET

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + Alpelisib for PIK3CA-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 11.0 months (95% CI 7.5–14.5) with alpelisib vs 5.7 months (95% CI 3.7–7.4) with fulvestrant alone (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.50–0.85; P < .001). Hyperglycemia 36.6% grade 3/4. [519]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fulvestrant + Capivasertib for PI3K Pathway-Altered HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second-line after prior AI ± CDK4/6 inhibitor

drugs.name: Fulvestrant
drugs.schedule: Days 1 and 15 of cycle 1, then Day 1 every 28 days

biomarkers.requirement: PIK3CA or AKT1 activating mutations or PTEN inactivating mutations and genomic changes

tier_1_regimens.notes: Overall PFS: 7.2 vs 3.6 months (HR 0.60; 95% CI 0.51–0.71). In PI3K pathway-altered: 7.3 vs 3.1 months (HR 0.50; 95% CI 0.38–0.65). Grade ≥3 adverse events: rash 12.1%, diarrhea 9.3%. Hyperglycemia any grade 16.3%. FDA-approved for PIK3CA, AKT1, or PTEN alterations. [520]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Elacestrant for ESR1-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second-line after prior AI ± CDK4/6 inhibitor

drugs.name: Elacestrant
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: In overall population, PFS: 2.79 vs 1.9 months (HR 0.69; P = .0018). In subgroup with prior ET + CDK4/6 inhibitor ≥12 months, median PFS 8.6 vs 1.9 months (HR 0.41; 95% CI 0.26–0.63). 72% had ESR1-mutated tumors. [528,529]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Imlunestrant + Abemaciclib for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second-line after prior AI ± CDK4/6 inhibitor

biomarkers.requirement: ESR1 mutation (most effective in ESR1-mutated, no prior CDK4/6 inhibitor)

tier_1_regimens.notes: Combination PFS: 9.4 vs 5.5 months (HR 0.57; 95% CI 0.44–0.73) vs imlunestrant alone. ESR1-mutated subgroup: median PFS 11.1 vs 5.5 months (HR 0.53; 95% CI 0.35–0.80). Single-agent imlunestrant improved PFS 5.5 vs 3.8 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.44-0.82) in ESR1-mutated. Grade ≥3 toxicities: 49% vs 17% with combination vs single agent. Most effective in ESR1 mutations and no prior CDK4/6 inhibitor. [6,530]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Vepdegestrant for ESR1-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: After prior endocrine therapy + CDK4/6 inhibitor in metastatic setting

drugs.name: Vepdegestrant
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: ESR1-mutated HR+/HER2- subgroup: PFS 5.0 vs 2.1 months (HR 0.58; 95% CI 0.43–0.78; P < .001). Overall population: PFS 3.8 vs 3.6 months (HR 0.83; 95% CI 0.69–1.01; P = .07), did not meet primary endpoint. Grade ≥3 AEs: 23.4% vs 17.6%. [18]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Everolimus + Exemestane for HR+/HER2- Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 11.0 vs 4.1 months (HR 0.38; 95% CI 0.31–0.48; P < .0001). Must meet BOLERO-2 criteria (progression within 12 months on nonsteroidal AI). If progression on everolimus, no data for additional everolimus regimen. Tamoxifen or fulvestrant + everolimus are also options. [523,524]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Docetaxel + Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 18.5 vs 12.4 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.51–0.75; P < .001). OS: 34% reduction in death risk at 30 months (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.52–0.84; P = .0008). Followed by maintenance pertuzumab + trastuzumab. If HR-positive: add endocrine therapy (AI ± palbociclib). [36,564,565]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Paclitaxel + Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line)

drugs.name: Paclitaxel
drugs.dose: 80 mg/m² weekly or 175 mg/m² every 21 days

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.dose: 4 mg/kg loading then 2 mg/kg weekly or 8 mg/kg then 6 mg/kg every 21 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: PERUSE study: median PFS comparable between docetaxel, paclitaxel, and nab-paclitaxel (19.6, 23.0, and 18.1 months). If HR-positive, add endocrine therapy (AI ± palbociclib) with trastuzumab ± pertuzumab as maintenance. [37,38,39,568]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki + Pertuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 40.7 vs 26.9 months (HR 0.46; 95% CI 0.44–0.71) vs THP. ORR 85% vs 79%. ILD/pneumonitis 12% (vs 1% THP), fatal AEs 3.4% vs 0.8%. Not preferred first-line for most patients due to ILD risk; considered for select patients including those with CNS involvement. [27,572]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Capecitabine/Tucatinib + Trastuzumab for HER2+ Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second/third-line and beyond metastatic (especially with CNS progression)
tier_1_regimens.notes: Preferred for both systemic and CNS progression. 1-year PFS rate: 33% vs 12% (HR 0.54; 95% CI 0.42–0.71); median PFS 7.8 vs 5.6 months. 2-year OS: 45% vs 27% (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.50–0.88). In patients with brain metastases, 1-year PFS 25% vs 0%. May also be given in second-line. [39,573]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki (T-DXd) for HER2+ Metastatic (Second/Third Line)
tier_1_regimens.notes: DESTINY-Breast02: PFS 17.8 vs 6.9 months (HR 0.36; 95% CI 0.28–0.45); OS 39.2 vs 26.5 months (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.50–0.86). May also be used in first-line for select patients with rapid progression within 6 months of neoadjuvant/adjuvant therapy. ILD/pneumonitis risk. [41,573,574]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Ado-Trastuzumab Emtansine (T-DM1) for HER2+ Metastatic (Second Line)
tier_1_regimens.notes: EMILIA: PFS 9.6 vs 6.4 months (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.55–0.77; P < .001); significant OS improvement (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.48–0.81; P = .0005). If not a candidate for fam-trastuzumab, T-DM1 may be used second-line. [40,576]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Capecitabine + Neratinib for HER2+ Metastatic

drugs.name: Neratinib
drugs.dose: 120 mg Days 1–7 then 160 mg Days 8–14 then 240 mg Days 15–21 (cycle 1); then 240 mg daily Days 1–21 from cycle 2

tier_1_regimens.notes: NALA: Risk of progression reduced by 24% (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.63–0.93; P = .0059). Fewer patients required CNS intervention. ORR 32.8% vs 26.7% (P = .1201). Diarrhea 83% any grade (24% grade 3/4). [55,589]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Chemotherapy + Margetuximab for HER2+ Metastatic (Fourth Line+)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 5.8 vs 4.9 months (HR 0.76; 95% CI 0.59–0.98). OS: 21.6 vs 21.9 months. Serious AEs comparable between groups. [56,590,591]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Chemotherapy + Pembrolizumab for TNBC (PD-L1 CPS ≥10, First-Line)
Sacituzumab Govitecan-hziy + Pembrolizumab for TNBC (First-Line)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 11.2 vs 7.8 months with chemo+pembrolizumab (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.51–0.84). ORR 60% vs 53%. No new safety concerns with combination. [25,599]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Sacituzumab Govitecan-hziy Monotherapy for TNBC (First-Line, CPS <10)
tier_1_regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1)

biomarkers.requirement: CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor

tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 9.7 vs 6.9 months with chemotherapy (HR 0.62; P < .001). Response duration 12.2 vs 7.2 months. PFS2: 18.2 vs 14 months (HR 0.70; 95% CI 0.55–0.90) despite crossover, supporting upfront use. [600]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Datopotamab Deruxtecan-dlnk for TNBC (First-Line, CPS <10)
tier_1_regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1)

biomarkers.requirement: CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor

tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 10.8 vs 5.6 months (HR 0.57; P < .0001). OS: 23.7 vs 18.7 months (HR 0.79; P = .029). No crossover in trial. Toxicity ≥ grade 3: 33% vs 29%. [15,563]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: PARP Inhibitor (Olaparib or Talazoparib) for gBRCA1/2 TNBC
tier_1_regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 and gBRCA1/2 PV)
tier_1_regimens.notes: OlympiAD (olaparib vs chemo): PFS 7.0 vs 4.2 months (HR 0.58; 95% CI 0.43–0.80; P < .001). EMBRACA (talazoparib vs chemo): PFS 8.6 vs 5.6 months (HR 0.54; 95% CI 0.41–0.71; P < .001). Available evidence suggests more effective earlier. Also indicated for platinum-sensitive TNBC. [9,17,643,645]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Platinum Monotherapy for gBRCA1/2 TNBC
tier_1_regimens.setting: First-line metastatic (PD-L1 CPS <10 and gBRCA1/2 PV)
tier_1_regimens.notes: TNT trial: In gBRCA1/2 mutant subgroup, ORR to carboplatin 68.0% vs docetaxel 33.3% (P = .03). PFS also improved with carboplatin. Somatic BRCA1/2 mutation carriers did not show the same advantage. [12,13,646]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Datopotamab Deruxtecan-dlnk for HR+/HER2- Metastatic (Second-Line+)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS benefit observed; 9-month PFS rates approximately double vs chemotherapy (37.5% vs 18.7%; HR 0.63). OS not statistically significant (HR 1.01; 95% CI 0.83-1.22; P = .9445). Not recommended after prior ADC. Benefit of use in patients with prior ADC treatment not known. [15,562,563]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Sacituzumab Govitecan-hziy for HR+/HER2- Metastatic (Second-Line+)
tier_1_regimens.notes: After prior endocrine therapy, CDK4/6 inhibitor, and at least 2 lines of chemotherapy. OS: 14.4 vs 11.2 months (HR 0.79; 95% CI 0.65–0.96; P = .020). ORR 21% vs 14%. Higher neutropenia, diarrhea, nausea, alopecia. [24,561]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for HR+/HER2-Low or HER2-Ultralow Metastatic (After Endocrine Therapy)
tier_1_regimens.site: Breast, invasive, HR+/HER2-low (IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH-) or HER2-ultralow (IHC 0+)

biomarkers.requirement: HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH negative (HER2-low) or IHC 0+ with membrane staining (HER2-ultralow)

tier_1_regimens.notes: HER2-low subgroup: PFS 13.2 vs 8.1 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.51–0.71) vs capecitabine or taxane chemotherapy. HER2-ultralow: PFS 13.2 vs 8.3 months (HR 0.78; 95% CI 0.50–1.21). OS differences not yet statistically significant. ILD/pneumonitis 11% (0.7% fatal). Not first-line for most patients with de novo metastatic disease given available alternatives. [560]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Pembrolizumab for MSI-H/dMMR Metastatic Breast Cancer
tier_1_regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.dose: 200 mg IV or 2 mg/kg every 21 days or 400 mg every 6 weeks
drugs.schedule: Until progression or up to 24 months

biomarkers.requirement: Microsatellite instability-high or mismatch repair deficient

tier_1_regimens.notes: FDA-approved for MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb) solid tumors after prior treatment with no satisfactory alternatives. [10,11,653,654]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Larotrectinib/Entrectinib/Repotrectinib for NTRK Fusion-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer
tier_1_regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives

drugs.name: Repotrectinib
drugs.dose: 160 mg daily Days 1–14 then 160 mg twice daily (cycle 1); then 160 mg twice daily continuously

biomarkers.requirement: NTRK gene fusion without acquired resistance mutation

tier_1_regimens.notes: FDA-approved for NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors without acquired resistance mutation or after progression following treatment. Repotrectinib may be used first-line (category 2B). [8,15,650,651,652]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Selpercatinib for RET Fusion-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer
tier_1_regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives
tier_1_regimens.notes: FDA-approved for RET fusion-positive solid tumors after prior systemic treatment or no satisfactory alternatives. [16,656,657]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Dostarlimab-gxly for MSI-H/dMMR Metastatic Solid Tumors
tier_1_regimens.setting: Any line after progression, no satisfactory alternatives

drugs.name: Dostarlimab-gxly
drugs.dose: 500 mg IV Days 1, cycles 1–4; then 1000 mg IV Day 1 every 42 days from cycle 5
drugs.schedule: Every 21 days for cycles 1–4 then every 42 days

biomarkers.requirement: Microsatellite instability-high or mismatch repair deficient

tier_1_regimens.notes: ORR 44% in dMMR/MSI-H tumors, complete response 13%. Median PFS 6.9 months, OS not reached. FDA-approved for adult patients with MSI-H/dMMR unresectable or metastatic tumors that progressed on/following prior treatment. [3,655]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Inflammatory Breast Cancer: Preoperative Anthracycline-Based Chemotherapy
tier_1_regimens.notes: NCCN Panel recommends preoperative systemic therapy with anthracycline-based regimen with or without taxanes for initial treatment of IBC. Complete planned chemotherapy prior to mastectomy. If not completed preoperatively, complete postoperatively. If HER2-positive, add trastuzumab for up to 1 year. [732,733,734]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Inflammatory Breast Cancer: Preoperative Chemotherapy + Trastuzumab (HER2+)
tier_1_regimens.site: Breast, invasive, inflammatory breast cancer, HER2-positive

drugs.name: Trastuzumab
drugs.schedule: Concurrent with chemotherapy, continue up to 1 year total

tier_1_regimens.notes: NOAH trial showed addition of trastuzumab to neoadjuvant chemotherapy significantly improved response rate and event-free survival in HER2-positive locally advanced/IBC. Trastuzumab continued for up to 1 year. A pertuzumab-containing regimen may be administered preoperatively for HER2-positive IBC (per Neosphere data). [414,743]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: IBC: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy (Post-Surgery, HR-Positive)
tier_1_regimens.setting: Adjuvant after mastectomy and chemotherapy completion

drugs.name: Endocrine therapy
drugs.schedule: Per standard schedule (tamoxifen 5 years or AI 5 years)

tier_1_regimens.notes: All patients with HR-positive IBC are recommended to receive endocrine therapy sequentially after completing the planned preoperative systemic therapy. Endocrine therapy options follow standard HR+/HER2-negative guidelines. [733]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Bone Metastases: Denosumab, Zoledronic Acid, or Pamidronate
tier_1_regimens.site: Breast, invasive, any subtype with bone metastases
tier_1_regimens.notes: Given in addition to systemic therapy if bone metastasis present, expected survival ≥3 months, and renal function adequate. Optimal zoledronic acid dosing every 12 weeks (ZOOM, CALGB 70604, OPTIMIZE-2 trials). Calcium and vitamin D supplementation required. Dental examination with preventive dentistry recommended prior to therapy. Denosumab superior to zoledronic acid for time to first SRE (HR 0.82; 95% CI 0.71–0.95; P = .01 for superiority). [487,488,489,492]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy: AI for 5 Years (Postmenopausal)
tier_1_regimens.notes: ATAC: fewer recurrences with anastrozole vs tamoxifen (HR 0.85; 95% CI 0.76–0.94; P = .003). BIG 1-98: superior DFS with letrozole vs tamoxifen (HR 0.81; 95% CI 0.70–0.93; P = .003). Three AIs have similar efficacy and toxicity. Consider extended therapy 7.5–10 years for high-risk node-positive patients. Baseline BMD recommended. [296,297,298,304,307]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy: Tamoxifen 5 Years (Premenopausal or Postmenopausal)

drugs.name: Tamoxifen
drugs.schedule: Daily for 5 years (consider extending to 10 years)

tier_1_regimens.notes: EBCTCG overview: tamoxifen decreases annual odds of recurrence by 41% and death by 31%. ATLAS: extending to 10 years reduces recurrence (HR 0.75 during years 10–14) and mortality. aTTom confirmed significant reduction in recurrence and death with 10 vs 5 years. [59,289,290,291,292]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: OFS + Exemestane (Premenopausal High-Risk HR+ Breast Cancer)

drugs.name: GnRH agonist (goserelin, leuprolide, or triptorelin)
drugs.dose: Goserelin 3.6 mg SC q4wk or 10.8 mg SC q12wk; Leuprolide 3.75–7.5 mg IM/SC q4wk or 11.25–22.5 mg q12wk; Triptorelin 3.75 mg IM q4wk or 11.25 mg IM q12wk

tier_1_regimens.notes: DFS 92.8% with OFS+exemestane vs 88.8% with OFS+tamoxifen (HR 0.66; P < .001). 15-year DFS 73.5% (OFS+exemestane) vs 70.5% (OFS+tamoxifen) vs 67.0% (tamoxifen alone). Considered for premenopausal patients with high-risk features: young age/longevity, high-grade tumor, lymph node involvement. 5 years optimal duration. [314,315,316]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Cytotoxic Chemotherapy Regimens for HR+/HER2- or HR-/HER2- Metastatic (Preferred Single Agents)

drugs.name: Paclitaxel
drugs.dose: 175 mg/m² IV Day 1 or 80 mg/m² IV weekly

drugs.name: Vinorelbine
drugs.dose: 25 mg/m² IV Day 1 weekly or 20–35 mg/m² Days 1 and 8 every 21 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: Sequential single agents preferred. Combination therapy only for rapid progression, high tumor burden, or visceral crisis. NCCN notes the selection/dosing/management is complex and requires experienced team. Cytotoxic therapy includes chemotherapy, ADCs, and targeted therapy. [BINV-Q 5 of 15]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin for TNBC Metastatic
tier_1_regimens.notes: Listed as other recommended regimen for HR+/HER2- and HR-/HER2- metastatic breast cancer. Platinum agents have demonstrated activity in metastatic TNBC. [BINV-Q 5 of 15]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for HER2-Low Metastatic (Second-Line After Prior Chemotherapy)
tier_1_regimens.setting: Second-line metastatic after 1-2 prior chemotherapy regimens
tier_1_regimens.notes: PFS: 9.9 vs 5.1 months (HR 0.50). OS: 23 vs 17 months (HR 0.69). HR+ subset: PFS 10.1 vs 5.4 months (HR 0.51; 95% CI 0.40–0.64), OS 23.9 vs 17.6 months (HR 0.69; 95% CI 0.55–0.87). ILD/pneumonitis 12%, fatal 0.8%. [26,560]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Imlunestrant for ESR1-Mutated HR+/HER2- Metastatic

drugs.name: Imlunestrant
drugs.schedule: Daily on Days 1–28, cycled every 28 days

tier_1_regimens.notes: Single-agent imlunestrant improved median PFS 5.5 vs 3.8 months (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.44-0.82, P < .001) among ESR1-mutated patients; benefit not seen in overall population. [6,530]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Endocrine Therapy + HER2-Targeted Therapy for HR+/HER2+ Metastatic (Maintenance After THP)
tier_1_regimens.setting: Maintenance after first-line chemo + THP in metastatic
tier_1_regimens.notes: PATINA: median PFS 44 months with palbociclib vs 29 months without (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.59–0.96). After taxane-based induction with THP, maintenance endocrine therapy (AI ± palbociclib) may be added to maintenance trastuzumab ± pertuzumab. Also supported by PERTAIN trial data. [571,596]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Abemaciclib + Fulvestrant + Trastuzumab for HR+/HER2+ Metastatic (Fourth Line+)
tier_1_regimens.notes: monarcHER: PFS 8.3 months with triple therapy vs 5.7 months (HR 0.67; 95% CI 0.45–1.0) vs trastuzumab + chemotherapy. ORR 33% vs 14%. Data immature for OS. [78]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Preoperative Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki Followed by THP for HER2-Positive Early Breast Cancer
tier_1_regimens.setting: Preoperative for high-risk early breast cancer

drugs.name: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki
drugs.schedule: Day 1, cycled every 21 days for 4 cycles

tier_1_regimens.notes: For HER2-positive breast cancer with high-risk of recurrence including lymph node positive (N1-3) disease or primary tumor stage T3-4 or inflammatory early breast cancer. Excludes patients with ILD/pneumonitis requiring steroids, ILD/pneumonitis at screening, or ECOG PS >1. [42]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Endocrine Therapy for HR+/HER2- Preoperative (Aromatase Inhibitor ± OFS)
tier_1_regimens.guideline_category: Preferred (for selected patients with comorbidities or low-risk luminal biology)
tier_1_regimens.notes: Preoperative endocrine therapy may be considered for patients with comorbidities or low-risk luminal biology based on clinical characteristics/genomic signatures. Optimal duration 4–6 months if response achieved. TransNEOS data showed correlation between 21-gene assay Recurrence Score and clinical response to preoperative letrozole. [258,260,268]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Endocrine + Olaparib for gBRCA1/2 PV After Preoperative Therapy (HR+/HER2-)
tier_1_regimens.setting: Adjuvant after preoperative therapy with residual disease or ≥4 positive lymph nodes
tier_1_regimens.notes: Adjuvant olaparib may be given concurrently with endocrine therapy after completion of RT. For those with ≥4 positive lymph nodes after adjuvant chemotherapy or residual disease after preoperative therapy. In patients eligible for both olaparib and abemaciclib/ribociclib, the optimal sequence is not known. [7,373]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Endocrine Therapy + CDK4/6 Inhibitor + Trastuzumab ± Pertuzumab for HR+/HER2+ Metastatic (First-Line Alternative)
tier_1_regimens.notes: PERTAIN: PFS 18.9 vs 15.8 months (HR 0.65; 95% CI 0.48–0.89) for pertuzumab+trastuzumab+AI vs trastuzumab+AI. Less toxic than chemotherapy-based options. TAnDEM: PFS 4.8 vs 2.4 months for AI+trastuzumab vs AI alone. Premenopausal patients require OFS. [592,596]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Fam-Trastuzumab Deruxtecan-nxki for HER2-Low Metastatic (After Prior Chemotherapy, Second-Line)
tier_1_regimens.notes: For those with HER2 IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH negative after 1-2 prior chemotherapy regimens. Based on DESTINY-Breast04. For HER2 IHC 0+, category 2A based on DESTINY-Breast06 (not yet showing OS benefit). [26,560]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Adjuvant Neratinib After Trastuzumab for HR+/HER2+ High-Risk
tier_1_regimens.notes: ExteNET: improved invasive DFS with neratinib after 1 year adjuvant trastuzumab (HR 0.73; 95% CI 0.57–0.92; P = .0083), primarily in HR-positive subgroup (HR 0.60; 95% CI 0.43–0.83; P = .063). Benefit/toxicities with neratinib after pertuzumab or ado-trastuzumab emtansine unknown. [i,39]

tier_1_regimens.regimen_name: Preoperative Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab followed by AC/Epirubicin + Pembrolizumab followed by Adjuvant Pembrolizumab for TNBC
tier_1_regimens.notes: KEYNOTE-522: preoperative carboplatin/paclitaxel + pembrolizumab followed by AC/Epirubicin + cyclophosphamide + pembrolizumab followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab. Cycles: 4 preoperative chemo cycles, then 4 adjuvant chemo cycles, then 9 cycles adjuvant pembrolizumab. Pembrolizumab can also be given at 400 mg or 4 mg/kg every 42 days. [5,273,274]

tier_2_handbook.definition: Breast cancer is a malignant neoplasm arising from the epithelial cells of the breast. According to the WHO Classification of Tumors, 5th Edition (2019), breast tumors are classified into in situ carcinomas and invasive carcinomas. The breast is an organ with a well-defined embryological origin from the mammary ridge, and malignancy can develop from any component of the ductal or lobular structures. The T category of the primary tumor is defined primarily by the size of the invasive component of the cancer, based on the maximum size of a tumor focus as an estimate of disease volume [ST-1]. T1 tumors measure 20 mm or less in greatest dimension (T1mi ≤1 mm, T1a >1–5 mm, T1b >5–10 mm, T1c >10–20 mm); T2 tumors measure >20 mm but ≤50 mm; T3 tumors measure >50 mm; and T4 tumors extend to the chest wall or skin, including T4d inflammatory carcinoma. Lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) is a benign entity and is removed from TNM staging in the AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th Edition [ST-1]. The diagnosis is confirmed by histopathologic examination with determination of hormone receptor status (ER and PR) and HER2 status, following standardized reporting protocols endorsed by the College of American Pathologists [BINV-1]. A clinical diagnosis of inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) requires erythema and dermal edema (peau d'orange) of a third or more of the breast skin, with pathologic confirmation of invasive cancer [IBC-1].

subtypes.name: Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS)
subtypes.description: Non-invasive malignancy confined to the milk ducts. DCIS is graded by nuclear grade (low, intermediate, high). It falls between atypical ductal hyperplasia and invasive ductal carcinoma within the spectrum of breast proliferative abnormalities [DCIS-1]. Over 50,000 cases of DCIS are diagnosed annually in the United States. Diagnosis is increased with screening mammography [MS-3].
subtypes.frequency: Over 50,000 cases per year in the United States [MS-3]
subtypes.key_associations: Screen-detected disease; ER-positive DCIS benefits from endocrine risk reduction therapy; high-grade DCIS more likely to progress to invasive disease; risk factors for local recurrence include larger DCIS, palpable mass, grade III disease, margins ≤2 mm, ER-negative tumors, and age <50 [DCIS-1]

subtypes.name: Encapsulated Papillary Carcinoma (EPC)
subtypes.description: Encapsulated papillary carcinoma without associated conventional invasion is staged as pTis because behavior is similar to DCIS per AJCC. Solid papillary carcinoma (SPC) should be specified as in situ or invasive based on WHO criteria; both forms have favorable outcomes [DCIS-1].
subtypes.key_associations: Favorable prognosis; staged as in situ when no invasion is present

subtypes.name: Invasive Breast Carcinoma of No Special Type (Ductal/NST)
subtypes.description: The most common invasive breast cancer histology. According to the WHO, carcinoma of NST encompasses multiple patterns including medullary pattern, cancers with neuroendocrine expression, and other rare patterns [BINV-4].
subtypes.key_associations: Treatment determined by HR and HER2 status; prognosis dependent on stage, grade, and molecular profile

subtypes.name: Invasive Lobular Carcinoma
subtypes.description: Second most common histologic subtype, characterized by single-file growth pattern of cells with loss of E-cadherin expression. MRI may be especially useful for defining extent of disease in lobular histology, which may be poorly delineated on mammography [MS-10].
subtypes.frequency: Second most common invasive histologic type
subtypes.key_associations: Often ER-positive; may be more difficult to detect on mammography; breast MRI recommended for evaluation; generally HR-positive/HER2-negative molecular subtype

subtypes.name: Mixed and Micropapillary Carcinoma
subtypes.description: Hybrid histologies with mixed features. Micropapillary carcinoma is a recognized histologic subtype with variable prognosis.
subtypes.frequency: Less common than ductal or lobular
subtypes.key_associations: Treatment follows general invasive breast cancer guidelines based on HR and HER2 status

subtypes.name: Metaplastic Carcinoma
subtypes.description: Rare histologic subtype. There are rare subtypes of metaplastic carcinoma (eg, low-grade adenosquamous and low-grade fibromatosis-like carcinoma) that have a favorable prognosis even without adjuvant systemic therapies [BINV-11]. More aggressive subtypes may behave similarly to triple-negative breast cancer.
subtypes.key_associations: May present as triple-negative; rare low-grade forms (adenosquamous, fibromatosis-like) have favorable prognosis; standard treatment follows triple-negative or HR-positive pathways based on receptor status

subtypes.name: Favorable Histologic Types
subtypes.description: Pure tubular, pure mucinous, pure cribriform, adenoid cystic, secretory carcinoma, and other salivary carcinomas. To be associated with favorable prognosis, the histologic type should not be high grade, should be pure (>90% as classified on surgical excision, not core biopsy alone), and should be HER2-negative. If atypical pathologic or clinical features are present, consider treating as ductal/NST [BINV-11].
subtypes.key_associations: Low-grade, ER-positive, HER2-negative; excellent prognosis; systemic therapy decisions based on HR status, tumor size, and nodal involvement; limited prospective data but endocrine therapy indicated for ER-positive disease

subtypes.name: HR-Positive, HER2-Positive Disease
subtypes.description: Breast cancers expressing both hormone receptors and HER2 overexpression. Represents a distinct therapeutic entity requiring both endocrine and HER2-targeted therapy.
subtypes.key_associations: HER2-targeted therapy with chemotherapy; endocrine therapy for HR component; pertuzumab-containing regimens for node-positive disease (APHINITY trial); improved pCR rates with dual HER2 blockade preoperatively

subtypes.name: HR-Positive, HER2-Negative Disease
subtypes.description: The most common molecular subtype, accounting for the majority of breast cancers. Endocrine therapy is the backbone of treatment, with chemotherapy reserved for higher-risk patients.
subtypes.key_associations: Endocrine therapy responsiveness; 21-gene recurrence score predicts chemotherapy benefit; CDK4/6 inhibitors in adjuvant and metastatic settings; PARP inhibitors for germline BRCA1/2 PV carriers; adjuvant abemaciclib or ribociclib for high-risk disease

subtypes.name: HR-Negative, HER2-Positive Disease (HER2-Overexpressing)
subtypes.description: Breast cancers overexpressing HER2 without hormone receptor expression. Highly responsive to HER2-targeted therapy.
subtypes.key_associations: HER2-targeted therapy is essential; trastuzumab-based regimens; pertuzumab for node-positive disease; fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki for residual disease; high response rates to neoadjuvant therapy

subtypes.name: Triple-Negative Breast Cancer (TNBC)
subtypes.description: Breast cancers lacking expression of ER, PR, and HER2. Often more aggressive with limited targeted therapy options. Represents a heterogeneous group.
subtypes.key_associations: Chemotherapy backbone; pembrolizumab for PD-L1 CPS ≥10 tumors; PARP inhibitors for germline BRCA1/2 PV carriers; sacituzumab govitecan and datopotamab deruxtecan-dlnk for metastatic disease; adjuvant capecitabine for residual disease after preoperative therapy; high pCR rates with preoperative immunotherapy

subtypes.name: Inflammatory Breast Cancer (IBC)
subtypes.description: Rare, aggressive form of breast cancer characterized by erythema and dermal edema (peau d'orange) of ≥1/3 of the breast skin. Staged as T4d. Represents approximately 1% to 6% of breast cancer cases in the United States [IBC-1].
subtypes.key_associations: Clinical-pathologic diagnosis; usually HR-negative, more frequently HER2-positive; requires preoperative systemic therapy followed by mastectomy and radiation; SLN dissection not reliable; poor prognosis; skin-sparing and nipple-sparing mastectomy contraindicated

subtypes.name: Paget Disease of the Breast
subtypes.description: Rare manifestation of breast cancer characterized by neoplastic cells in the epidermis of the nipple-areolar complex (NAC). Most commonly presents with eczema, bleeding, ulceration, and itching of the nipple. Associated cancer elsewhere in the breast in up to 80% to 90% of cases [PAGET-1].
subtypes.key_associations: Nipple eczema/ulceration/bleeding; associated underlying breast cancer in 80–90% of cases; requires full-thickness skin biopsy of involved NAC; breast MRI may identify additional disease

subtypes.name: Phyllodes Tumors
subtypes.description: Rare tumors comprised of both stromal and epithelial elements. Exist in benign, borderline, and malignant subtypes. Occur at mean age of 40 years, younger than typical breast carcinomas [PHYLL-1].
subtypes.key_associations: Rapid growth; large size (>3 cm); difficult to distinguish from fibroadenoma on biopsy; treatment is surgical excision with tumor-free margins ≥1 cm; local recurrence is the most common site; axillary staging not necessary unless clinically involved

tier_2_handbook.molecular_pathogenesis: Breast cancer is a molecularly heterogeneous disease driven by diverse genomic alterations. Germline BRCA1/2 pathogenic variants (PVs) are found in approximately 5% of all breast cancer patients, with higher rates among those with HER2-negative disease [BINV-Q 2, 7]. These mutations impair homologous recombination DNA repair, creating sensitivity to PARP inhibitors such as olaparib (median PFS 7.0 vs 4.2 months; HR 0.58; P < .001) and talazoparib (median PFS 8.6 vs 5.6 months; HR 0.54; P < .001) in the metastatic setting [MS-83]. PIK3CA activating mutations occur in approximately 40% of hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancers and are targetable with PI3K pathway inhibitors; the INAVO120 trial demonstrated improved PFS with fulvestrant/inavolisib/palbociclib versus fulvestrant/placebo/palbociclib (15.0 vs 7.3 months; HR 0.43; 95% CI 0.32–0.59) [BINV-Q 6, MS-63]. ESR1 mutations are frequently detected after exposure to aromatase inhibitors and are targetable with elacestrant, imlunestrant, or vepdegestrant [BINV-Q 6]. AKT1 activating mutations and PTEN inactivating mutations/genomic changes are targetable with capivasertib/fulvestrant (PFS 7.2 vs 3.6 months overall in CAPItello-291) [BINV-Q 6, MS-65]. Germline PALB2 PVs confer sensitivity to olaparib (ORR 75% in a phase II study) [MS-83]. NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions, though rare, are targetable with larotrectinib, entrectinib, or repotrectinib [BINV-Q 7]. Microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) tumors respond to pembrolizumab or dostarlimab-gxly [BINV-Q 7]. RET gene fusions are targetable with selpercatinib [BINV-Q 7]. High tumor mutational burden (≥10 mut/Mb) predicts response to pembrolizumab [BINV-Q 7]. HER2 (ERBB2) activating mutations, distinct from HER2 amplification, are targetable with neratinib/trastuzumab/fulvestrant (ORR 39% in the SUMMIT trial) or tucatinib/trastuzumab [BINV-Q 8]. Somatic BRCA1/2 mutations may also predict olaparib benefit (ORR 33% in germline non-BRCA1/2 HR-related gene carriers) [BINV-Q 8]. FGFR1-3 gene fusions/mutations are emerging targets for erdafitinib [BINV-Q 8]. Gene expression profiling reveals distinct molecular subtypes: the 21-gene recurrence score (Oncotype DX) stratifies risk and predicts chemotherapy benefit in HR-positive/HER2-negative disease, with TAILORx demonstrating no chemotherapy benefit for recurrence scores 0–10 and RxPONDER showing chemotherapy benefit for premenopausal patients with scores up to 25 [BINV-N]. The 70-gene (MammaPrint), 50-gene (Prosigna/PAM50), and 12-gene (EndoPredict) assays provide prognostic information [BINV-N]. The Breast Cancer Index predicts benefit from extended endocrine therapy [BINV-N].

epidemiology.incidence: Breast cancer is the most common malignancy in females in the United States. According to the American Cancer Society, an estimated 313,510 Americans will be diagnosed with breast cancer in the United States in 2024 [MS-2]. Over 50,000 cases of DCIS of the female breast are diagnosed annually in the United States [MS-3]. Inflammatory breast cancer accounts for approximately 1% to 6% of breast cancer cases in the United States [IBC-1].
epidemiology.mortality: An estimated 42,780 Americans will die of breast cancer in the United States in 2024 [MS-2]. Breast cancer is second only to lung cancer as a cause of cancer death in females [MS-2]. The 15-year breast cancer mortality rate after DCIS diagnosis is approximately 1.7% for those treated with breast-conserving therapy versus 2.3% for BCS alone (HR 0.77; 95% CI 0.67–0.88; P < .001) [MS-4].
epidemiology.trends: The diagnosis of DCIS has increased since the introduction and increased utilization of screening mammography [MS-3]. Treatment strategies continue to evolve with advances in targeted therapy, immunotherapy, and de-escalation approaches.
epidemiology.demographics: Breast cancer occurs predominantly in females. Male breast cancer is uncommon, and recommendations are generally extrapolated from data in females, with specific considerations including increased genetic testing, preference for tamoxifen over aromatase inhibitors as monotherapy, and bone density monitoring [BINV-J]. Breast cancer in pregnancy is infrequent (1.3 per 10,000 live births) but is associated with more advanced stage, larger tumors, poorly differentiated histology, higher rate of ER/PR negativity, and approximately 30% HER2-positivity [MS-91]. Age at diagnosis varies by subtype, with phyllodes tumors having a mean age of 40 years [MS-90].

risk_factors.detail: Found in approximately 5% of all breast cancer patients. Associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer and hereditary breast cancer syndrome. Genetic counseling recommended for patients at high risk as defined by NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment [BINV-1]. BRCA1/2 PV carriers have higher rates among HER2-negative disease [BINV-Q 2].
PALB2 PVs confer breast cancer susceptibility. Olaparib has shown activity in patients with germline PALB2 PV (ORR 75%, clinical benefit rate 83.3%, median PFS 9.4 months) [BINV-Q 7, MS-83].

Known genetic predisposition to breast cancer. Patients with Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53 mutation) have an increased risk of phyllodes tumors [PHYLL-1]. TP53 PV carrier is a relative contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (category 2B) [BINV-G].
Homozygous (biallelic) inactivation for ATM mutation often leads to ataxia-telangiectasia syndrome and is an absolute contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (category 2B) [BINV-G].

Young age is a significant predictor of increased likelihood of ipsilateral breast tumor recurrence after breast-conserving treatment [MS-13]. Patients ≤35 years or premenopausal carriers of known BRCA1/2 mutation should consider additional risk reduction strategies [MS-13].
risk_factors.strength: Moderate (increased local recurrence risk; consideration for risk reduction)

risk_factors.detail: Breast cancer occurring concurrently with pregnancy is associated with ALN-positive disease, larger primary tumor size, poorly differentiated histology, more frequent ER/PR negativity, and approximately 30% HER2-positivity. Diagnosis is often delayed [MS-91].

Obesity (BMI ≥30) is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in women with ER-positive tumors. Obesity is associated with poorer outcomes for certain subtypes of breast cancers [MS-53].

Smoking is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer. Smoking increases perioperative complications for all types of breast reconstruction including wound healing, mastectomy skin flap necrosis, implant exposure, and infection [MS-26].
Alcohol consumption is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in women with ER-positive tumors [MS-53].
Menopausal hormone therapy is relatively contraindicated in survivors of hormonally mediated cancers; should be used with caution in those with increased genetic cancer risk [BINV-17].

Higher breast density may affect detection and may warrant additional screening with breast MRI. Patients with personal history of breast cancer diagnosed at age ≤50 or with dense breasts should consider annual MRI [BINV-B].

protective_factors: Active lifestyle — evidence suggests that active lifestyle may lead to optimal breast cancer outcomes [BINV-17]. A prospective study of 1,490 patients with stage I–III breast cancer showed an association between physical activity and improved survivorship, regardless of obesity [MS-54].

Healthy diet — high fruit and vegetable consumption is associated with improved survivorship in early-stage breast cancer [MS-54]. The WINS trial showed potential OS benefit with low-fat diet in ER-negative disease [MS-54].
Limited alcohol intake — limiting alcohol consumption reduces risk of contralateral breast cancer [BINV-17].
Maintaining ideal body weight (BMI 20–25) — the NCCN Panel recommends an active lifestyle and ideal body weight (BMI 20–25) for optimal overall health and breast cancer outcomes [BINV-17].

Adjuvant bisphosphonate therapy — in postmenopausal patients with high-risk node-negative or node-positive tumors, adjuvant bisphosphonate therapy reduces risk of distant metastasis (3–5 years) [BINV-16, MS-44].

management_principles.overview: The management of breast cancer requires a multidisciplinary approach tailored to the tumor's clinical stage, biological subtype, and patient-specific factors. Treatment is guided by the principles of maximizing cure while minimizing treatment-related morbidity and preserving quality of life. A multimodal strategy typically incorporates surgery, radiation therapy, and systemic therapy (endocrine, cytotoxic, targeted, or immunotherapy). The choice and sequencing of these modalities depend on the disease's extent, tumor biology (hormone receptor [HR] and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 [HER2] status), genomic risk, and patient preferences. All treatment decisions should be made through shared decision-making within a multidisciplinary tumor board. NCCN recognizes the importance of clinical trials and encourages participation when applicable and available.

by_intent.patient_group: Patients with non-metastatic (stage 0, I, II, III) invasive or non-invasive breast cancer.
by_intent.approach: Aggressive multimodality treatment aimed at eradicating all disease. This typically includes local therapy (surgery ± radiation) to achieve locoregional control and systemic therapy (endocrine, chemotherapy, targeted, immunotherapy) to reduce the risk of distant recurrence.

by_intent.patient_group: Patients with non-metastatic disease receiving systemic therapy after (adjuvant) or before (neoadjuvant) definitive local therapy.
by_intent.approach: Systemic therapy is administered to eliminate micrometastatic disease and reduce recurrence risk. Neoadjuvant therapy may also be used to downstage tumors for breast conservation or to provide in vivo chemosensitivity information.

by_intent.patient_group: Patients with recurrent, unresectable, or metastatic (stage IV) disease.
by_intent.approach: Systemic therapy is administered to prolong survival, control symptoms, and maintain or improve quality of life. Treatment selection is guided by tumor biology, prior therapies, performance status, and patient goals. Local therapies (surgery, radiation) may be used for symptom control or in select oligometastatic scenarios.

management_principles.mdt_requirements: The guidelines are developed and updated by a multidisciplinary panel of representatives from NCCN Member Institutions with expertise in medical oncology, surgical oncology, radiation oncology, pathology, and reconstructive surgery. Optimal patient care requires a multidisciplinary team (MDT) approach for evaluation and treatment planning. A multidisciplinary approach is especially important in the management of breast cancer recurrence to consider all potential treatment options.
management_principles.performance_status_guide: Patient performance status is a critical consideration, particularly in the palliative setting. Most patients with metastatic disease will be candidates for multiple lines of systemic therapy. At each reassessment, clinicians should assess the value of ongoing treatment, the risks and benefits of an additional line of systemic therapy, patient performance status, and patient preferences through a shared decision-making process. The potential side effects of additional lines of therapy may outweigh any clinical benefit in a patient who has a compromised performance status.

management_pathways.title: Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS), Tis, N0, M0

options.label: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS) without lymph node surgery
options.recommendation: If margins are negative and RT is planned. SLNB may be considered in select circumstances (e.g., concern for persistently positive margins, excision compromising future SLNB).

options.label: Total mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) ± reconstruction
options.recommendation: Indicated for patients not candidates for BCS or those who choose it. Omission of SLNB in the setting of mastectomy may be considered in patients >50 years with non-palpable low-grade ER-positive DCIS with no abnormal axillary findings and in patients in whom the primary reason for mastectomy is patient preference.

options.label: Whole breast radiation therapy (WBRT)
options.recommendation: Following BCS reduces ipsilateral breast tumor recurrence (IBTR) rates by 50%–70%. Recommended for most patients after BCS.

options.label: Accelerated partial breast irradiation (APBI)/Partial breast irradiation (PBI)
options.recommendation: Considered for appropriately selected patients who are low risk. May be considered for patients meeting all RTOG 9804 criteria or ASTRO 'suitable' criteria for APBI.

options.label: No RT (endocrine therapy alone)
options.recommendation: Omission of RT and treatment with endocrine therapy alone may be considered if all RTOG 9804 criteria are met (screen-detected, grade 1–2, size ≤2.5 cm, margins ≥3 mm).

options.label: Endocrine therapy for risk reduction
options.preference: Category 1 for those undergoing BCT followed by RT; Category 2A for those undergoing excision alone
options.recommendation: Consider for patients with ER-positive DCIS treated with BCS. Tamoxifen for premenopausal patients. Tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor for postmenopausal patients. Low-dose tamoxifen (5 mg/day or 10 mg every other day) is an option if standard-dose is not tolerated.

adjuvant_logic.summary: After primary treatment (surgery ± RT), patients with ER-positive DCIS should be considered for endocrine therapy to reduce the risk of ipsilateral and contralateral breast cancer events.

management_pathways.title: Invasive Breast Cancer, cT1-3, cN0 or cN+, M0: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS) Pathway

options.label: BCS ± surgical axillary staging (category 1)
options.recommendation: SLNB is recommended for staging. Omission of SLNB may be considered in select patients >50 years with cT1N0, HR+/HER2-negative, grade 1–2 tumors who are agreeable to receiving whole breast RT and endocrine therapy, based on SOUND and INSEMA trials. Caution should be used in those with lobular histology.

options.label: Whole breast RT ± boost to tumor bed
options.recommendation: Strongly recommended after BCS. Dose: 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions (preferred hypofractionated). Boost of 10–16 Gy in 4–8 fractions recommended for higher-risk features. Comprehensive regional nodal irradiation (RNI) should be considered for central/medial tumors, T3 tumors, or T2 tumors with other high-risk features.

adjuvant_logic.summary: Systemic therapy is determined after surgery based on final pathology and biologic subtype (see separate pathways). RT is typically given after systemic therapy if indicated.

management_pathways.title: Invasive Breast Cancer, cT1-3, cN0 or cN+, M0: Mastectomy ± PMRT Pathway

options.label: Nipple-sparing, skin-sparing, or total mastectomy with surgical axillary staging (category 1) ± reconstruction
options.recommendation: SLNB or ALND based on clinical nodal status. Reconstruction options should be discussed.

options.label: Post-mastectomy radiation therapy (PMRT)
options.preference: Category 1 for node-positive or positive margins
options.recommendation: Recommended for patients with positive nodes (pN1mi with other risk factors, pN1a, pN2-3) or positive surgical margins (re-excision preferred). For pN0/pN0[i+] and ≤pT2 (≤5 cm) with negative margins, PMRT is not routinely indicated. PMRT to chest wall + comprehensive RNI (including any undissected axilla at risk) is indicated for pN2-3, any ypN+, and cT4 any N.

adjuvant_logic.summary: Systemic therapy is determined after surgery. RT is typically given after completion of systemic chemotherapy if indicated.

management_pathways.title: Adjuvant Systemic Therapy: HR-Positive, HER2-Negative, Postmenopausal Patients (pT1-3, pN0 or pN+)

options.label: Consider adjuvant endocrine therapy (category 2B)
options.recommendation: To reduce risk of second primary and small benefit in recurrence reduction.

nodes.criteria: pT1b-T3 (>0.5 cm) and pN0 or pN1mi (≤2 mm axillary node metastases) or pN1 (1-3 positive nodes). Determine if candidate for chemotherapy.

options.label: Strongly consider 21-gene Recurrence Score (RS) RT-PCR assay (category 1)
options.recommendation: To guide chemotherapy decision. RS ≥26: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy. RS <26: Adjuvant endocrine therapy (category 1).

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy
options.recommendation: If candidate for chemotherapy and RS high. See BINV-M for preferred regimens.

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy
options.recommendation: For all HR-positive disease. Consider adjuvant abemaciclib or ribociclib for eligible patients (see BINV-K 3 of 4 for eligibility criteria).

options.label: Adjuvant abemaciclib or ribociclib
options.recommendation: Consider for eligible patients with high-risk features (e.g., ≥4 positive lymph nodes, or 1-3 positive lymph nodes with grade 3 or tumor size ≥5 cm).

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy (category 1)
options.recommendation: Systemic adjuvant chemotherapy is indicated. Select patients may be eligible for treatment with adjuvant abemaciclib or ribociclib and/or adjuvant olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV.

management_pathways.title: Adjuvant Systemic Therapy: HR-Positive, HER2-Negative, Premenopausal Patients

options.label: Consider adjuvant endocrine therapy (category 2B)
options.recommendation: Consider for risk reduction.

nodes.criteria: pT1b-T3 (>0.5 cm) and pN0. Determine if candidate for chemotherapy. Strongly consider 21-gene RT-PCR assay.

options.label: Recurrence score ≤15
options.recommendation: Adjuvant endocrine therapy ± ovarian suppression/ablation. Consider adjuvant ribociclib for eligible patients. Chemotherapy may not be needed.

options.label: Recurrence score 16–25
options.recommendation: Consider chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy ± OFS/ablation. Alternatively, consider OFS combined with tamoxifen or an aromatase inhibitor.

options.label: Recurrence score ≥26
options.recommendation: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy ± OFS/ablation. Consider adjuvant ribociclib for eligible patients.

nodes.criteria: pN1mi or pN1 (1-3 positive nodes). Determine if candidate for chemotherapy. Consider gene expression assay to assess prognosis.

options.label: Adjuvant endocrine therapy ± ovarian suppression/ablation
options.recommendation: For all HR-positive disease. Consider adjuvant abemaciclib or ribociclib for eligible patients.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy followed by endocrine therapy ± ovarian suppression/ablation
options.recommendation: If candidate for chemotherapy. Consider adjuvant abemaciclib or ribociclib and/or adjuvant olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV.

management_pathways.title: Adjuvant Systemic Therapy: HR-Negative, HER2-Positive Disease

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy with trastuzumab (category 2B)
options.recommendation: Benefit of HER2-targeted therapy is uncertain in this small tumor size group not studied in randomized trials. Decision must balance toxicities.

nodes.criteria: pT1b (0.6-1.0 cm) and pN0 or pN1mi

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy with trastuzumab
options.recommendation: Based on higher risk of recurrence for small HER2-positive tumors compared to HER2-negative of same size.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy with trastuzumab (category 1) ± pertuzumab for pT2-T3
options.recommendation: Standard of care. TCH (Docetaxel/Carboplatin + Trastuzumab) is a preferred regimen, especially for those at risk for cardiac toxicity.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy with pertuzumab + trastuzumab (category 1, preferred)
options.recommendation: Updated APHINITY trial data confirm long-term benefit of adding pertuzumab for node-positive disease.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy with trastuzumab (category 1)
options.recommendation: Alternative option.

management_pathways.title: Adjuvant Systemic Therapy: HR-Negative, HER2-Negative (TNBC) Disease

options.label: No adjuvant therapy
options.recommendation: For very low-risk disease. In select patients with high-risk features (e.g., young patients with high-grade histology), adjuvant chemotherapy may be considered (category 2B).

nodes.criteria: pT1b-pT3 (>0.5 cm) and pN0 or pN1mi

options.label: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy and adjuvant olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV
options.recommendation: Standard adjuvant chemotherapy regimens (see BINV-M). Olaparib given after completion of RT.

options.label: Adjuvant chemotherapy (category 1) and adjuvant olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV
options.recommendation: Standard adjuvant chemotherapy. Olaparib given after completion of RT. If pembrolizumab was given preoperatively, consider adjuvant pembrolizumab.

management_pathways.title: Management of Bone Metastases in Recurrent/Stage IV Disease

nodes.criteria: Bone metastases present, expected survival ≥3 months, adequate renal function

options.label: Add bone-modifying agent (denosumab, zoledronic acid, or pamidronate) (category 1)
options.recommendation: In addition to systemic therapy and/or endocrine therapy. Use with calcium and vitamin D supplementation. Optimal dosing of zoledronic acid is every 12 weeks. Patients should undergo a dental examination with preventive dentistry prior to initiation.

management_pathways.title: Systemic Treatment of Recurrent Unresectable or Stage IV (M1) Disease: ER- and/or PR-Positive; HER2-Negative

options.label: Endocrine therapy + CDK4/6 inhibitor (preferred first-line)
options.recommendation: AI + CDK4/6i (ribociclib, abemaciclib, or palbociclib) for postmenopausal or premenopausal patients receiving OFS. Fulvestrant + CDK4/6i if prior endocrine therapy or relapse within 12 months. For PIK3CA activating mutations: Fulvestrant/Inavolisib/Palbociclib.

options.label: Cytotoxic therapy (see BINV-Q)
options.recommendation: Chemotherapy or ADCs. Endocrine therapy combinations with targeted therapies may be considered in certain circumstances with visceral crisis.

management_pathways.title: Systemic Treatment of Recurrent Unresectable or Stage IV (M1) Disease: ER- and/or PR-Positive; HER2-Positive

options.label: Cytotoxic therapy + HER2-targeted therapy (preferred)
options.recommendation: Docetaxel + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab (category 1) with maintenance Pertuzumab + Trastuzumab. If HR-positive, add endocrine therapy (Aromatase inhibitor ± Palbociclib) + Trastuzumab ± Pertuzumab. Paclitaxel + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab is also preferred. Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki + Pertuzumab is other recommended.

management_pathways.title: Systemic Treatment of Recurrent Unresectable or Stage IV (M1) Disease: ER- and PR-Negative; HER2-Negative (TNBC)

options.label: Chemotherapy + Pembrolizumab or Sacituzumab govitecan-hziy + Pembrolizumab
options.recommendation: Albumin-bound Paclitaxel, Carboplatin/Gemcitabine, or Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab. Sacituzumab govitecan-hziy + Pembrolizumab is also preferred.

nodes.criteria: First-line, PD-L1 CPS <10 or not candidate for PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor therapy and no germline BRCA1/2 PV

options.label: Sacituzumab govitecan-hziy or Datopotamab deruxtecan-dlnk or Systemic chemotherapy
options.recommendation: Sacituzumab govitecan-hziy and Datopotamab deruxtecan-dlnk are category 1 preferred. Chemotherapy is category 2A.

items.text: Complete history and physical examination with attention to breast and regional lymph nodes.
Assess for distress using NCCN Distress Thermometer and Problem List.
Pathology review of all breast cancer specimens.
Determination of estrogen receptor (ER) and progesterone receptor (PR) status by IHC.
Determination of HER2 status by IHC and/or ISH per ASCO/CAP guidelines.
Consider Ki-67 testing if HR-positive, HER2-negative, and considering adjuvant abemaciclib.
Axillary ultrasound if considering omission of surgical nodal staging.
Breast MRI (optional), with special consideration for mammographically occult tumors.
Chest diagnostic CT ± contrast, abdomen ± pelvis diagnostic CT with contrast or MRI with contrast, bone scan or sodium fluoride PET/CT if signs/symptoms of metastatic disease or clinically high risk.
items.recommendation: Category 2A for CT; Category 2B for bone scan/PET
If candidate for preoperative or adjuvant systemic therapy

items.text: Comprehensive metabolic panel, including liver function tests (LFTs) and alkaline phosphatase.
items.recommendation: If candidate for preoperative or adjuvant systemic therapy

items.text: Genetic counseling and testing if patient is at risk for hereditary breast cancer, has triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) at any age, or is a candidate for adjuvant olaparib.
Assess for germline BRCA1/2 pathogenic variants (PVs) in all patients with recurrent or metastatic breast cancer to identify candidates for PARP inhibitor therapy.
Address fertility and sexual health concerns as appropriate. Refer to fertility specialist if future pregnancy desired.
Pregnancy test in all patients of childbearing potential (if pregnant, see PREG-1).
Consider MGPT (somatic and germline) to identify candidates for targeted therapies in the metastatic setting.

surgery.role: Primary local therapy for non-metastatic breast cancer with curative intent. May also be used for palliation in metastatic disease.
principles: The choice of surgery is based on patient preference, tumor size, tumor location, breast size, and contraindications to radiation.
Breast-conserving surgery (BCS) is equivalent to mastectomy with regard to overall survival for stage I and II breast cancer (category 1).
Achieving pathologically negative margins is essential for BCS. For invasive cancer, a positive margin is defined as 'ink on tumor'.
Axillary lymph node staging is critical for prognosis and treatment planning. Sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) is the preferred method for axillary staging in clinically node-negative patients.
Reconstruction options should be discussed with all patients undergoing mastectomy. Reconstruction does not impact oncologic outcomes.

procedures.name: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS)
procedures.approach: Lumpectomy or partial mastectomy with goal of achieving negative margins. Oncoplastic techniques may be used for optimal cosmesis.
procedures.indication: Most patients with early-stage invasive breast cancer or DCIS who are candidates for adjuvant radiation therapy.
procedures.key_complications: Positive margins requiring re-excision, infection, poor cosmesis.

procedures.name: Mastectomy
procedures.approach: Total, skin-sparing, or nipple-sparing mastectomy with surgical axillary staging. Delayed or immediate reconstruction may be performed.
procedures.indication: Patients not candidates for BCS, those with contraindications to RT, or patient preference.
procedures.key_complications: Surgical site infection, bleeding, seroma, poor cosmesis, complications of reconstruction (capsular contracture, implant failure, flap necrosis).

procedures.name: Axillary Lymph Node Staging (SLNB vs. ALND)
procedures.approach: SLNB is the preferred method for staging clinically node-negative patients. ALND (Level I/II) is reserved for patients with clinically suspicious nodes or positive SLNB not meeting criteria for omission.
procedures.indication: Standard staging procedure for most patients with invasive breast cancer undergoing surgery.

surgery.resectability_criteria: For BCS, absolute contraindications include: inflammatory breast cancer or extensive skin/dermal lymphatic involvement; diffuse suspicious microcalcifications; inability to achieve negative margins (defined as no ink on tumor); homozygous ATM mutation (category 2B); gestational breast cancer unable to receive RT within 12–16 weeks; and multicentric disease with certain criteria (category 2B). Relative contraindications include: known genetic predisposition, TP53 PV, active connective tissue disease, prior RT to the area, and pregnancy.
surgery.pre_operative_optimisation: Accurate clinical staging prior to surgery is critical. For patients considering preoperative systemic therapy, additional workup includes axillary assessment (ultrasound ± biopsy), breast MRI (optional), and consideration of gene expression assays. Coordination with plastic surgery for reconstruction planning should occur prior to surgery.

radiation_therapy.role: Local therapy to reduce the risk of locoregional recurrence after BCS or mastectomy. It is an integral component of curative treatment for most patients with invasive breast cancer.
principles: Individualize RT planning and delivery. Use CT-based treatment planning.
Whole breast RT is typically delivered with hypofractionated schedules (preferred).
A boost to the tumor bed is recommended in patients at higher risk for recurrence.
Regional nodal irradiation (RNI) is recommended based on nodal status and tumor factors.
Sequencing of RT with systemic therapy: RT typically follows chemotherapy if indicated.
For patients with a history of RT to the breast, re-irradiation may be considered with careful assessment of prior dose and volume.

dose_frameworks.name: Whole Breast RT (Hypofractionated)
dose_frameworks.indication: Preferred for most patients after BCS.

dose_frameworks.name: Whole Breast RT (Ultra-Hypofractionated)
dose_frameworks.schedule: 28.5 Gy given once weekly; 26 Gy given daily
dose_frameworks.indication: Select patients with pTis/T1/T2/N0 aged >50 years after BCS. Optimal fractionation for boost delivery unknown.

dose_frameworks.name: Tumor Bed Boost
dose_frameworks.indication: Recommended for higher-risk features (age <50, high-grade, close/positive margins).

dose_frameworks.name: APBI/PBI (External Beam)
dose_frameworks.schedule: 30 Gy: every other day; 40 Gy: daily
dose_frameworks.indication: Appropriately selected low-risk patients meeting ASTRO criteria.

approaches.name: Post-BCS Radiation
approaches.concurrent_chemotherapy: Concurrent administration with CMF-based chemotherapy may be considered. RT typically follows other chemotherapy regimens.
approaches.indication: All patients after BCS for invasive cancer or DCIS unless contraindicated.
approaches.key_trial: EBCTCG meta-analysis: reduces 10-year risk of recurrence (19% vs 35%).
approaches.toxicities: Acute: skin erythema, fatigue, breast edema. Late: breast fibrosis, telangiectasia, cosmetic changes, rare rib fractures, cardiac/pulmonary toxicity with left-sided RT.

approaches.name: Post-Mastectomy Radiation Therapy (PMRT)
approaches.dose_fractionation: 45–50.4 Gy in 25–28 fractions or 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions to chest wall
approaches.concurrent_chemotherapy: RT typically follows chemotherapy. Concurrent endocrine therapy is acceptable.
approaches.indication: Patients with positive nodes (pN1mi with other risk factors, pN1a, pN2-3), positive surgical margins, or large tumors (T3/T4).
approaches.key_trial: EBCTCG meta-analysis: reduces 20-year breast cancer mortality in patients with 1-3 positive nodes.
approaches.toxicities: Acute: skin erythema, desquamation, fatigue. Late: arm lymphedema, shoulder stiffness, breast fibrosis, cardiac/pulmonary toxicity.

approaches.name: Regional Nodal Irradiation (RNI)
approaches.dose_fractionation: 46–50 Gy in 23–25 fractions or 39–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions
approaches.concurrent_chemotherapy: Given with whole breast or chest wall RT.
approaches.indication: Node-positive disease (≥4 positive nodes strongly recommended; 1-3 positive nodes may be considered based on risk factors). May be considered for node-negative with high-risk features (e.g., central/medial tumors).
approaches.key_trial: MA.20: improved DFS with RNI. EORTC 22922: reduced breast cancer mortality with 15-year follow-up.
approaches.toxicities: Increased risk of lymphedema, pneumonitis, brachial plexopathy, cardiac toxicity.

systemic_therapy.overview: Systemic therapy includes endocrine therapy, cytotoxic chemotherapy, targeted therapy, and immunotherapy. The choice depends on tumor biology (HR, HER2 status), stage, prior therapies, and patient factors. In the curative setting, therapy aims to eliminate micrometastatic disease. In the metastatic setting, therapy aims to prolong survival and control symptoms.

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Dose-dense AC followed by Paclitaxel, TC, or chemotherapy with olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV. Endocrine therapy (tamoxifen or AI ± OFS) ± CDK4/6 inhibitor (abemaciclib or ribociclib) for high-risk.
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: To reduce recurrence risk. Gene expression assays (21-gene) guide chemotherapy decisions in node-negative and node-positive disease.
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Other recommended: AC every 3 weeks (category 2B), EC, TAC, CMF. Useful in certain circumstances: carboplatin/taxane regimens.
by_stage_and_setting.biomarker_requirements: HR testing (ER/PR). 21-gene assay for prognosis and chemotherapy prediction. Germline BRCA1/2 testing for olaparib eligibility.

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: TCHP (Docetaxel/Carboplatin + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab) followed by adjuvant trastuzumab ± pertuzumab. For residual disease: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki (high risk) or Ado-trastuzumab emtansine.
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: Dual HER2 blockade improves pCR and outcomes. HER2-targeted therapy for 1 year is standard.
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: AC followed by Docetaxel + Trastuzumab, Paclitaxel + Trastuzumab (low-risk T1N0).

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: Stage II-III: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab (preop) followed by AC + Pembrolizumab (preop) then Pembrolizumab (adj). Adjuvant olaparib if germline BRCA1/2 PV. Adjuvant capecitabine or pembrolizumab for residual disease.
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: Immunotherapy improves outcomes in PD-L1-positive TNBC. Platinum enhances pCR in BRCA-mutated tumors.
by_stage_and_setting.biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS testing (for pembrolizumab). Germline BRCA1/2 testing.

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: First-line: AI + CDK4/6 inhibitor (ribociclib, abemaciclib, palbociclib) or Fulvestrant + CDK4/6 inhibitor. For PIK3CA mutation: Fulvestrant/Inavolisib/Palbociclib.
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: CDK4/6 inhibitors significantly improve PFS and OS in first-line setting.
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Fulvestrant + alpelisib or capivasertib (for PIK3CA/AKT1/PTEN alterations). Everolimus + endocrine therapy. ESR1-targeted therapies (elacestrant, imlunestrant). Cytotoxic therapy for visceral crisis.
by_stage_and_setting.biomarker_requirements: ESR1 mutation testing at progression (ctDNA preferred). PIK3CA/AKT1/PTEN testing.

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: First-line: Docetaxel/Paclitaxel + Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab (THP) with maintenance Trastuzumab ± Pertuzumab. If HR-positive, add endocrine therapy. Second-line: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki.
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: Dual HER2 blockade is standard first-line. Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki shows superior efficacy in second-line.
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: Capecitabine/Tucatinib + Trastuzumab (especially with CNS metastases). Ado-trastuzumab emtansine.
by_stage_and_setting.biomarker_requirements: HER2 testing. Consider germline BRCA1/2 testing.

by_stage_and_setting.preferred_regimen: First-line, PD-L1 CPS ≥10: Chemo + Pembrolizumab or Sacituzumab govitecan-hziy + Pembrolizumab. First-line, PD-L1 CPS <10: Sacituzumab govitecan-hziy, Datopotamab deruxtecan-dlnk, or chemotherapy.
by_stage_and_setting.rationale: Immunotherapy benefits PD-L1-positive TNBC. ADCs show significant activity.
by_stage_and_setting.alternatives: PARP inhibitors (olaparib, talazoparib) for germline BRCA1/2 PV. Platinum chemotherapy for germline BRCA1/2 PV.
by_stage_and_setting.biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS testing (22C3 antibody). Germline BRCA1/2 testing. HER2 testing (low/ultralow for fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki eligibility).

key_regimens.name: Dose-Dense AC followed by Paclitaxel
